what_it_does: Defines a phone-sized HTML explainer screen titled "A Unit, In Its Constellation" with lead copy, a generated hub-network visual, five action rows for the V, and a call-to-action to open the live face.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Links shared piece and phone stylesheets, then adds file-local CSS for the teaching layout, lead text, visual area, rows, triangle markers, and text styling.
Uses a phone shell with status bar, page heading, explanatory lead copy, visual container, section label, five action rows, action button, and hatch element.
Lists five actions: Navigate, Annotate, Ask, Drive, and Open the source.
Loads /surface/runtime/organisms.js and injects a DNA.org.hubNetwork SVG/HTML visual into #vis.
The generated visual uses an octagon hub with a "{ }" glyph and five labeled surrounding nodes: entry, test, cli, types, and extract.
standouts: The file frames a unit as a gold-centered constellation of related nodes.
The explanatory copy connects the visual metaphor to company memory and drill-down navigation.
The V section turns the screen into a compact feature explanation rather than only a static visual.
conventions: Uses shared surface CSS classes such as screen, sbar, p-hd, p-sup, p-h, sec, p-act, and p-hatch.
Uses dna design tokens for spacing, type, color, and lines.
Keeps the page self-contained with inline CSS and a small inline initialization script.
Uses short prefixed local classes under g- for the explainer-specific layout.
concerns: The inline script assumes DNA.org.hubNetwork is available after loading /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
The generated visual is injected with innerHTML, so rendering depends on the runtime helper returning trusted markup.
The visual labels are example-like and are not explained beyond appearing as role nodes.
notes: A phone explainer page that teaches a unit face as a hub-and-neighbor constellation with five available actions.
questions: What does "the V" stand for from this file alone?
Are the node labels intended as fixed examples or placeholders?
What behavior should "Open the Live Face" trigger?
What does the gold center specifically represent beyond "the thing itself"?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents a readable phone explainer for a unit constellation and the actions available from it.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make an abstract unit face understandable by pairing a constellation visualization with concise descriptions of what a user can do from that face.
novelty: The page treats a single known item as a live face with neighboring role nodes and action verbs, presented in a compact phone teaching layout.
contribution: Contributes a neutral explainer substrate for representing an entity as a centered constellation, with a reusable pattern of lead copy, generated relationship visual, action rows, and a live-face call-to-action.
summary_for_embedding: Phone-sized HTML explainer page for a unit face, combining shared surface styling, a runtime-generated hub-network constellation, five action descriptions, and a live-face CTA.
intention_for_embedding: Designed to teach the concept of a unit in a company's memory by showing it as a central node surrounded by related nodes and paired with available interactions.
